items.title: Heals
items.summary: I have always considered myself a poet. A warrior poet. But…
items.content_html:
I have always considered myself a poet.

A warrior poet.

But I don't write poetry.

And I don't war.

I'm a man of peace, remember?

I just ramble.

Rant.

Muse.

To amuse myself.

And no one else.

Although I do sometimes rhyme.

Slow, sometimes quick.

Billy Gates (can we still quote him?) used to tell Warren Buffett how lucky he was being born in modern times. That if he were born in another era he would be bait for dinosaurs or something worse.

I chuckle at that for two reasons. For the truth of what he said and also for the irony. 

We live in an era of where college students about to graduate boo their billionaire commencement speakers for talking about ai.

And where the billionaire keeps on talking.

If I could just finish my thought...

Boo!

The pro wrestling fan in me knows that getting boos ain't all that bad.

That the worst thing you can get is a yawn.

A look at the time.

A concession stand break.

A visit to the can.

Turn the channel.

The idea is, if you can't be loved, it's best to be despised.

Ain't that something?

It's something.

In wrestling, bad guys are "heels" and good ones are "babyfaces."

As a kid, you love the faces.

As a young adult, you love the heels.

As an old(ish) man, you love the memories.

Old faces talk about how much they miss the "pops" (the crowd cheer).

Heels talk about the time getting stabbed in Norfolk and almost causing a riot.

Both with the biggest of grins.

It ain't just Billy and Warren, ya know.

I love being born when I was born.

I love this timeline.

This era.

This now.

I don't know how I'd do versus a dinosaur.

But I'm thankful I don't have to worry about that.

For now.

Mostly for the dinosaur.

Booooooooooooooo!!!

[Cheshire cat grin.]

I've always wanted to be a babyface.

But let's face it.

I've always been a heel.

And if you can't be loved...

🐯

Whoooooooooooooooooo!

items.title: Dumb Complex
items.summary: Never confuse complexity with superiority. Simplicity is the goal. Basic. Elegant.
items.content_html:
Never confuse complexity with superiority.

Simplicity is the goal.

Basic.

Elegant.

Graceful.

You know how chefs get all fancy and make normal sheet ridonkulous?

The fancier the price tag, the more crazy things get.

And smaller portions, too.

Shaving frozen duck liver fat onto vanilla ice cream churned by an elf and putting it into a thimble to serve to you, as you eagerly wait with a toothpick sized spoon in your hand.

That sounds delightful.

Listen:

Cast iron pan.

A nice chunk of tri tip.

Sizzle.

Butter.

Cut into steaks.

Salt.

Eat.

Don't get too crazy focusing on the seasoning.

Work the meat!

Serve with rice and kimchi.

Eat.

Let settle.

Conquer world.

After nap.

Conquer world before or after nap.

Up to you.

It's not keep it simple, stupid.

It's keep it stupid simple.

items.title: Amigo
items.summary: One of the best dudes I know was a Blackhawk helicopter…
items.content_html:
One of the best dudes I know was a Blackhawk helicopter pilot.

By the time I knew him, he was a 1L in law school.

But he and I quickly bonded over the repetitive lifting of heavy objects.

And we quickly became good friends.

He loved to quote a movie I'd never seen.

Backdraft.

It's a movie about firefighters firefighting and sheet.

Apparently, there's a part where the two main characters are fighting a fire that overtakes them.

One gets into dire trouble and the other holds onto him for dear life.

The one in peril tells the other to leave him, to let him go, to get out and get to safety.

And the line my buddy quotes, is the other guy's response:

"You go, we go."

He was the ultimate got-your-back, share-a-foxhole, dude.

A good man.

A badmf.

How many of those are left?

Have the honor of knowing many.

But like all old(ish) men, we have lost touch over the years.

My fault (as most things are of this ilk).

But I remember the line.

You go...

🚁

And so I ask, are you like that?

And with who?

Who's your "You go?"

And does anyone "You go" about you?

items.title: Snooze Button
items.summary: I'll keep this short and sweet. Because I have no idea…
items.content_html:
I'll keep this short and sweet.

Because I have no idea what I'm going to say.

So how are youuuuuuuu?

How's life?

Zzzzzzzzzzz.

items.title: Wrong In All The Right Ways
items.summary: If your operating system has been wrecked before, any new issues…
items.content_html:
If your operating system has been wrecked before, any new issues make you wonder.

Is this related to that big one?

Often, it is unrelated.

But you look through a different filter once it happens.

Everything is post-wreck view.

And so it is, with many of us, with tread on our tires.

Our reality.

Any time something goes awry, we tend to blame the big one.

It's a lens I wear now.

Old folks used to say things like "My bum knee is acting up again."

And as a youngin, you wouldn't understand.

It was almost funny.

And as an old(ish) man, well my bum knee acts up.

Not all the time, you see.

Just when it's in a mood to act up.

And that's interesting right?

We are a system onto ourselves.

Often cooperating, but constantly fighting.

Cycling.

Iterating.

Good days.

Bad days.

Every day, a good 'un.

Sometimes, you have to kill a part of you, so the rest can live.

I'm talking literally and figuratively, here.

As I am wont to do.

I have.

You have, too.

Or will, if you live long enough.

To everyone who's still out there kickin' after taking a lickin'.

And if you find yourself wrapping your hands for yet another fight, know that I'm in your corner.

Well, more likely, at home on my couch, eating delicious Korean food, watching you on the big screen.

And rooting.

This Coke Zero's for you.

🍻

There is no tomorrow.

Fight for it anyway.

items.title: Put Me In, Coach
items.summary: I have nothing to say today. Go get 'em, tiger. Oh,…
items.content_html:
I have nothing to say today.

Go get 'em, tiger.

Oh, a hat tip to the Harbaugh brothers' father:

I want you to attack this day with an enthusiasm previously unknown to mankind!

Have a great day.

🐯

items.title: Family Jewels
items.summary: Bas Rutten is a hero of mine. I don't have too…
items.content_html:
Bas Rutten is a hero of mine.

I don't have too many of those.

But Bas is one.

And, he made a series of MMA videos back in the day.

Even a how to protect yourself in the streets video.

It's both hilarious and educational.

Highly recommend.

Anyways, in this particular self-defense video he goes on and on about the virtues of a kick to the groin.

How simple it is.

How effective.

How we should use it.

Everybody underestimates a kick to the groin.

Meaning, all things being unequal, a kick to the nuggets tends to even things out.

May the nads be ever in your favor.

Now, as a old(ish) man of peace(ish), I would never tell you to kick someone in the chicken tenders, but do so if you need to.

Do so if you must.

And that's a good rule right?

Fight honorably.

Until you need to not.

If you're in a struggle that seems like you winning means you get to live, remember to fight dirty from the start.

What's fighting dirty?

Well, basically take a look at what you cannot do in an organized, professional, fight.

And do a whole lotta that.

Piledriving. Eye-gouging. Headbutts. Throat. Using objects. Leverage. Back of the head.

Read the list of no-no's and make 'em yes-yes's.

Only in defense of course.

When lives are at stake.

Yours or your loved ones.

And only if you are at a disadvantage.

Fight fair until it's not.

Oh, for many, it's never fair.

🦶🍒

Ring the bells.

items.title: Lost and Found
items.summary: Why do they call it fall in love? Shouldn't it be…
items.content_html:
Why do they call it fall in love?

Shouldn't it be rise?

Rise in love?

Soar in love?

No, fall's right.

Love is a strength and a weakness.

I've said this before.

And I'll say it again.

Love is a strength and a weakness.

It makes you stronger and weaker at the same time.

When you love something or someone, you have a duality at play.

An infinite helix of affection intertwined with concern.

Joy and sorrow.

Jubilance at birth.

Mourned at death.

Everything in between in between.

Love is the most powerful thing in the universe.

More powerful than hate.

You've been powered by both.

What say you?

So you have a choice:

To love or not to love.

And for many years, I chose the latter.

So I would remain strong.

But that was weakness talking.

Love's weakness is one I cannot put into words.

It can cripple you.

But the alternative is loneliness.

Emptiness.

Void.

Sure, it can all end the same way.

But better to have loved, I say.

Than to never have loved at all.

I'm missing something in the middle part.

But you understand.

May you know the love of loving someone more than yourself.

May you know the love of having been loved by someone more than they love themselves.

And may those loves fall in love.

And may you all always believe it was better to have done so.

Better to have loved...

items.title: 'Neath The Sunshine
items.summary: What do grandmasters do with their idle time? Their down time.
items.content_html:
What do grandmasters do with their idle time?

Their down time.

Their let their hair down time.

They probably meditate a lot.

Contemplate.

Ever tinkering on their grandmastery.

Do you remember the old show, The People's.Court?

It was Judge Judy and all them before all them.

Judge Wapner.

Judge Wapner was an old curmudgeon.

I am partial to them.

I remember one episode years ago where someone was on the show and kept calling Judge Wapner, "Your Majesty," instead of Your Honor.

"Yes, your majesty."

"No, I'm not royalty, call me your honor."

"I'm sorry your majesty, I mean, your honor!"

He kept doing it. Slipping up.

Which infuriated Judge Wapner at first, then he just found it funny.

Anger, then delight.

Which is, as we all know, the sweet spot.

I suppose I could find the clip online somewhere but I don't want to.

Because I know I'll have remembered a detail wrong or got the whole show wrong.

So I'll leave it in my brain.

As a childhood memory.

Hope all you mothers had a good day yesterday. 

And all days, while we're counting.

And we're always counting.

🎵 "One for sorrow, two for joy..." 🎵

items.title: Word To Your Mother
items.summary: Happy Mother's Day. To you if you're a mother. And to…
items.content_html:
Happy Mother's Day.

To you if you're a mother.

And to your mother.

Word.

And the mothers around you.

🤶
